Konstantin Nomerotski

Scientific Software Engineer | Computational Chemistry | Software for Chemistry

Scientific software engineer specializing in computational workflows, molecular simulation infrastructure, and distributed systems for drug discovery.

New York, NY | k-nom@outlook.com | k-nom.com | github.com/kon-218 | linkedin.com/in/k-nom

Education

University of Bristol

Master of Science with Honors in Chemistry with Scientific Computing – 3.8 GPA

Sept 2020 – Jul 2024

Activities & Achievements: Thesis grade 76%, First Class Honors

Relevant modules: Core Chemistry, Scientific Computing, Data Science, High-Performance Computing, Models and Predictions, Mathematics

Thesis project: Efficient Calculation of Photoabsorption Cross-Sections for Volatile Organic Compounds

Technical Skills

Scientific Computing & Cheminformatics: Python, RDKit, OpenMM, OpenFF, OpenFE, MDTraj, ORCA

Workflow & Infrastructure: Flyte, Ray, Docker, RabbitMQ, Redis, Celery, GitHub Actions

Backend & Distributed Systems: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Microservices, REST APIs, Docker Swarm

Frontend: React, Next.js, TypeScript

Scientific & HPC: SLURM, MPI, OpenMP, Linux, Bash

Featured Projects

Ligand-X: Automated Protein Simulation Platform

Full-Stack Scientific Applications / Cheminformatics

2025 – Present

  • Developed a comprehensive microservices platform for structural, physical, and chemical analysis of ligand-protein complexes, streamlining computer-aided drug discovery workflows.
  • Designed and implemented a distributed microservices architecture utilizing FastAPI, Celery, RabbitMQ, and Redis to orchestrate computationally intensive simulations, supporting asynchronous task orchestration for GPU-intensive workloads.
  • Integrated 10+ state-of-the-art computational chemistry tools into isolated Docker containers, including OpenMM, AutoDock Vina, OpenFF, OpenFE, and ORCA.
  • Built a responsive frontend using React, Next.js, TypeScript, Mol* 3D visualization, and Ketcher molecular editor for seamless interactive data analysis.

Distributed Higgs Boson Decay Processing

Distributed Systems & Cloud Computing | Python, Docker, RabbitMQ

Oct 2023 – Dec 2023

  • Redesigned a monolithic ATLAS open data processing script into a high-performance, containerized microservices architecture.
  • Deployed and orchestrated distributed processing tasks locally using Docker Swarm to achieve dynamic container scaling.
  • Implemented RabbitMQ to enable decoupled, reliable, and asynchronous communication between task publishers and consumer workers, preventing race conditions.

Atmospec Open Source Contributor: Photolysis Rate Visualization

Scientific Software | Python, NumPy, Bokeh

Jan 2023 – May 2023

  • Collaborated on a development team to design and integrate an interactive photolysis rate visualization module into the AtmoSpec Jupyter web app.
  • Deployed and packaged the analytical module within containerized environments for seamless distribution and cross-platform reproducibility.

Portfolio Website

Web Development | GH Pages, HTML, Jekyll, SCSS

Oct 2024 – Present

  • Designed and built a custom portfolio website to showcase scientific software projects, interactive visualizations, and technical blog posts.
  • Implemented continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) pipelines using GitHub Actions.

Work Experience

Scientific Software Engineer - Intern

Examol Corporation – New York City

Feb 2025 – May 2025

  • Developed cloud-native molecular simulation workflows for binding free energy calculations and lead optimization in distributed drug discovery infrastructure.
  • Implemented an automated workflow for calculating absolute free binding energy of small molecules using Python, Flyte, Git, Docker, and Azure VM.
  • Designed and built containerized pipelines for equilibration and simulation of protein-ligand complexes using OpenMM, OpenFF, AmberTools, and MDTraj.

Research Associate

Insilico Photochemistry Group – University of Bristol

Sept 2023 – Jun 2024

  • Developed an automated workflow using Python, Bash, and SLURM scripts, reducing runtime by 80% and enabling efficient in silico UV-vis spectrum calculation.
  • Implemented data processing and visualization pipelines using Pandas and Matplotlib for large datasets.

Additional Information

  • Fluent in English and Russian
  • US Citizen
